title: Dalea stanfieldii
taxonomy: Dalea stanfieldii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Dalea, which consists of approximately 100 species of shrubs and herbs. Dalea stanfieldii is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is often found in dry, sandy areas and rocky outcrops. The classification of Dalea stanfieldii is further supported by its characteristic flowers, which are typically pink or purple in color.

intro: Dalea stanfieldii, also known as Stanfield's prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
